definitions: A member of a people of mixed Frankish and Scandinavian origin who settled in Normandy and became a dominant military power in medieval Europe.
Relating to Normandy or the Normans.
A style of Romanesque architecture developed by the Normans in England.
examples: The Norman Conquest of England occurred in 1066.
Norman is a common name in many English-speaking countries.
The church features impressive Norman arches.
Many Norman castles still stand today.
